acceptance_targets: [ch16-craft-001] The revised handoff passes only if a reader can point to one explicit beat before the copy starts that either (a) Omar has been notified about the archive's contents, or (b) Marisol states a concrete reason his material can be preserved without prior notice despite the objection she raises at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:109. If the scene still depends on the reader hand-waving that gap away, fail.

[ch16-booker-001] The revised chapter passes this point only if lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9-61 contain one explicit statement of the three-path dilemma, and every later return to the choice in that span adds a new material stake or obstacle rather than rewording the same menu. A reader should be able to name what changed at each beat.

[ch16-prose-001] The revised passage passes only if no more than two major emotional turns in the lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:55-61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:141 still rely on an explicit negation-then-replacement pattern. The others must land without that formula.

[FBR-02-006] Pass if `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5783`-`snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5789` shows Leela beginning with an evasive or partial answer and then correcting herself under Marisol's pressure, without using 'almost said' or a close variant. Fail if the passage still depends on the same suppression formula heard earlier.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised handoff passes only if a reader can point to one explicit beat before the copy starts that either (a) Omar has been notified about the archive's contents, or (b) Marisol states a concrete reason his material can be preserved without prior notice despite the objection she raises at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:109. If the scene still depends on the reader hand-waving that gap away, fail.
findings.evidence: Marisol refuses blind custody because the archive can hurt both other grad workers before they hear it from Leela, but the copy starts after June is notified and Omar still is not. See snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:127, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:131.
findings.problem: The chapter's pivotal handoff scene states one custody rule and executes another. Marisol explicitly balks at taking a sealed archive that can blow back on June and Omar before either hears it from Leela, but the scene only dramatizes notice to June before preservation proceeds. Because Omar's visa-sensitive letter trail is still in the manifest, the scene leaves a causality hole in exactly the place where chain of custody and informed risk are supposed to become concrete.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:131 so the procedure is internally coherent. Either put Omar on-page before the copy begins, or give Marisol a specific legal/strategic reason June notice is enough for immediate preservation while Omar's materials are quarantined, deferred, or otherwise handled differently.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter passes this point only if lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9-61 contain one explicit statement of the three-path dilemma, and every later return to the choice in that span adds a new material stake or obstacle rather than rewording the same menu. A reader should be able to name what changed at each beat.
findings.evidence: Leela's leave/bargain/expose menu is spelled out repeatedly rather than escalated through new pressure. See snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:61.
findings.problem: The opening half keeps restating the same strategic triangle in abstract terms: leave quietly, bargain for a smaller future, or turn the archive over. That gives the aftermath weight, but by the third and fourth restatement the chapter is rephrasing its own decision rather than sharpening it. The result is slower than the intended aftermath rhythm because several paragraphs summarize the choice instead of changing it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the repeated option-setting across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:49, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:61. Keep one explicit articulation of the three paths, then let later beats earn movement through fresh pressure such as June's recruiter offer, Mohit's second email, or the actual threshold of counsel custody.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised passage passes only if no more than two major emotional turns in the lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:55-61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:141 still rely on an explicit negation-then-replacement pattern. The others must land without that formula.
findings.evidence: The narration repeatedly uses the same negation-then-redefinition move: 'There was nothing cruel in it. That made it worse.' 'Waiting for her to come back emotionally was not a plan. It was a superstition with a charging cable.' 'Crying was too organized a word for it. The cold just made the face honest.' 'She did not feel brave. She felt used up.' See snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:55, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:141.
findings.problem: That corrective rhythm is sharp the first time, but at this density it starts to read like a default sentence engine. Because the chapter is otherwise controlled and observant, the repeated 'not X, actually Y' turn becomes a prose tic that makes multiple emotional beats land with the same manufactured snap.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework at least two of the corrective turns around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:55, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:141 so the shift lands through image, bodily action, or syntax variation rather than the same negation/redefinition formula.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5783`-`snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5789` shows Leela beginning with an evasive or partial answer and then correcting herself under Marisol's pressure, without using 'almost said' or a close variant. Fail if the passage still depends on the same suppression formula heard earlier.
findings.problem: This is another recurrence of the same four-chapter 'almost said' suppression pattern (chapters 6, 7, 16, and 18). In chapter 16, where Marisol is explicitly forcing completeness, the repeated template weakens the scene's stripped-down clarity. The chapter wants a harsher, less familiar resistance move than another swallowed sentence cue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5783`-`snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5789` so Leela's resistance surfaces through a sharper partial answer or bodily stall, not another 'almost said' beat. This is the point in the book where her evasions should sound more cornered and less stylistically familiar.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the absurdity and cruelty of graduate labor without converting the book into caricature or chase-plot menace. In edge cases, prefer the messier and more compromising version of events if it yields stronger human consequence and remains causally plausible; render sex, professional theft, bureaucratic harm, and emotional pettiness with the same specificity as any technical problem.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns vague at the exact point humiliation, erotic charge, class shame, visa fear, or institutional cruelty becomes concrete, rewrite until the cost is materially and socially legible.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High; contractions are the baseline in speech, and uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate precision, class performance, emotional emphasis, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public and private pressure, ensemble chapters and solitary ones, bureaucratic scenes and domestic scenes, deadline compression and aftermath inventory, so no two adjacent chapters rely on the same structural rhythm or dialogue density.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary English grounded in material detail; technical language should appear only when the social reality of the lab requires it, and it should never replace bodily or relational consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person centered on Leela, with occasional brief widenings that register group mood and institutional atmosphere without leaving her interpretive frame.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed observational lines with longer accrual sentences that can carry comedy, shame, and procedural dread in the same movement; quiet chapters should breathe, deadline chapters should tighten without turning telegraphic.
